With the wheat harvest COM
pleted and fall coming on, the an.
nual crop of Migrants has been
keeping the Union Rescue mission
officials on the jump. Jobs are ex-
ceedingly scarce and about rs per
cent of the applicants are young
men under 25 years of age, which is
an almost Unprecedented condition.
A bottle of hair tonic, jar of
cold cream and a complexion oint-
ment was the result of recent
petty raid on Ritter's drug store by
an old man who is thought to be
mentally unbalanced.


At a meeting of the members of
the Hays Park Baptist mission and
the Minnehaha Baptist church
Thursday night, plans were formu-
lated for selling the property of
both congregations. combining the
funds for the purchase of a lot in a
more central location.


Exhibits from Alberta and British
Columbia for the Dry Farming Con-
gress, to be held in October at the
Interstate fair, will approximate
three carloads of grains and fruits,
is the assurance of officials of the
exposition. The Canadian exhibit is
to be big feature of the congress.
Residents living near Illinois ave-
nue. and Hamilton street have asked
the police for protection from a
gang OF young rowdies who are ter,
rorizing the neighborhood.


Alleging that his wife intends to
go the pace that kills in spite Ct all
he can do. Gordon Dodd filed suit
for divorce in the superior court
yesterday.


At a meeting OF the Sunday
School Superintendent union at
the Y. M. c. A. last night, plans
were outlined for the go day,
double up" membership campaign.


II you have noticed a new face
among the officers of the Y, NJ. c.
4. lately. it probably belongs to Mr..
q. A. Forbes. the new assistant
